one_time_description: Prior year subsidy settlements of $35,241 recorded as adjustment to operations
economic_notes: COVID-19 caused approximately $2,400,000 decrease in gas and variety store sales. First Nation received approximately $531,000 in additional COVID-19 funding. Construction contract for new highway service centre with value of $5,300,000 entered into during the year, approximately $1,800,000 expended. Interests in multiple Feed-in-Tariff (FiT) solar/hydro partnerships carried at nil due to unavailable financial statements.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to obtain financial statements for Feed-in-Tariff government business partnerships (Hanover Solar, 5868 Orr Lake, 7550 Lasalle, Minten SFN, Boost Power II, Solblack SFN, Hay Bay Solar, Great West Energy, Great West Energy II), all carried at nil. Five subsidiaries consolidated (Shawanaga Retail GP Inc., Shawanaga Retail LP, SFN Cann Retail GP Inc. inactive, SFN Ec Dev Corporation inactive, 1582787 Ontario Ltd. inactive). One GBE accounted for under modified equity method: Shwe Miikaan Limited Partnership (33.333% interest). GBE investment balance $260,396. Funding repayable to various government agencies totals $477,964. Subsequent to year end, FNFA interim financing of $11,457,520 converted to long-term loan. No schedule of remuneration provided in audited statements. Community operations segment includes housing and infrastructure; expenses_housing reported as 0 as not separately broken out. COVID-19 significantly impacted store sales (~$2.4M reduction).
